SignalScope.definition: SignalScope maps evolving multi-domain systems into nested bounded participation windows, where each window expresses relative participation, coupling, imbalance, flow, and residue under a conserved sum constraint.

core_principle.name: conserved_participation
core_principle.rule: At every valid scope level, all channels have values and sum to 1.
core_principle.zero_interpretation: A channel may approach effective zero participation, but it is not undefined.

local_window.meaning: bounded relational state inside one scope window

meta_window.meaning: lifted state of the system as a whole

recursive_lift.description: Three constrained windows can define a higher constrained window. Three global windows can define a meta window using the same rule.
recursive_lift.not_identity_warning: Higher levels are not identical to lower levels; they are renormalized projections.

steps: clamp or rectify raw values to non-negative domain
if S > floor: W = X / S
if S <= floor: use fallback policy

coupling.meaning: degree of simultaneous participation across domains

curvature.meaning: turning or switching in state trajectory

residue.meaning: memory trace of prior state evolution

flow_projection.meaning: coarse vector-field direction over the participation manifold

single_channel_collapse.meaning: one domain dominates while others are suppressed

stable_attractor.condition: low variance in W over a window OR repeated trajectory loop

decoupling.condition: C(t) below coupling_floor for N frames

recoupling.condition: C(t) rises above recoupling_threshold after decoupling

chaotic_flicker.condition: high hue variance AND high direction entropy

top_down.formula: W_i'(t+1) = Normalize((1 - beta) * W_i(t+1) + beta * G(t))

consistency_condition.rule: Higher levels must be derivable from lower levels, and lower levels must remain admissible under higher-level constraints.

admissibility.definition: Allowed region of the participation manifold.
rules: coupling may have floors or thresholds
certain regions may be forbidden or discouraged depending on application
admissibility.meaning: not every next state is allowed; continuation is constrained

ontology_mapping.epsilon: E_scope(t), imbalance or deviation from balanced participation
ontology_mapping.rho: C(t), coupling or sustaining participation capacity
ontology_mapping.recoupling: C(t) rises after suppressed shared participation
ontology_mapping.zero: effective floor or symmetry limit, not undefined absence

brainwave_eeg.warning: SignalScope does not read thoughts; it tracks participation dynamics.

implementation_requirements: Keep raw feature extraction separate from SignalScope normalization.
Every valid frame must produce W values.
Never allow undefined channels inside the scope.
Use floor thresholds for practical zero detection.
Preserve local, global, and meta levels separately.
Do not collapse levels unless explicitly requested.
Log both raw feature values and normalized participation values.
Support recursive lift from local to global to meta.
Support top-down feedback from higher windows to lower windows.
Expose event detections as structured labels, not only colors.
SignalScope.final_statement: SignalScope is a recursively liftable state-dynamics instrument: local windows, global windows, and meta windows all obey the same conserved participation law, allowing complex systems to be observed as nested trajectories of coupling, imbalance, residue, and flow.
